The Facebook, absolutely. It's like I like to call it the Google. The Google, yes. So, I mean, you know, people actually do care. Everyone thinks everyone else's life is more interesting than their own, and they like to live, many people like to live, you know, kind of vicariously through others. You can even go as far back into, you know, the 30s, 40s, 50s. That was the whole reason for soap operas, you know, to watch these other people's lives that were all more interesting than your own, and a lot of people enjoyed watching that. So, you've got to have the personal stuff in there, and it doesn't have to be about your last vacation. There's a lot of different ways to open up to someone. We see Dan do it all the time. If you have followed Dan Kenney for any period of time, there are certain things we all know about him. We know that he loves to harness race, and he races a couple hundred times a year. Which, you know, nightly, I say a little prayer that that man does not get hurt or injured severely, a little selfishly, but, you know, we all know that he goes out and harness races. We know he loves Disney. He thinks they are like the cats meow, and we all know that about him. We know that he's been married a couple of times, and if you know the story, on the third time he married Rich, which is personally, I think, one of his more entertaining stories. You know, so we all know these things about him, and it's not super personal. I mean, we know he only flies private, which is great. I guess I've never flown private, but I assume it's great. But, you know, we know these things about him, and we know some of his personality. He's a little grumpy, but we connect with things. If you've been around for a long time, you know, at one point he was bankrupt in his career, and that was a long, long, long time ago, but if you've ever been bankrupt, that's definitely something you connect with Dan on, and you know the struggles and the feeling of that, and that strengthens your bond with him. And so you want to give people reasons to connect with you. So in my newsletter, it's business to business, right? So we don't talk a ton about, you know, the last vacation I went on, unless there's a story that's relevant, but I do talk a lot about, I let people know certain things about me. One, I do, I also go to Disney a lot and really enjoy Disney. I have five kids, and they're all boys, you know, and, you know, there's a bunch of other things that I use to connect with people, and I just use them in my storytelling, and this builds relationships, and I get comments on those all the time. Literally, I mean, assuming I'm taking phone calls that day, you know, I will typically have someone comment on something they read in my newsletter, you know, and that's just the norm of it because they feel they built the relationship. So that personal side is really, really important, and it will really help make or break referrals. So that's one piece. Second set of content, you need to improve the life of the reader. So that's a huge mistake like we were talking about with the auto shop or a dentist where they don't want to, they just want to talk about what they do, and at the end of the day, if you're a dentist and you're, you know, most dentists, their average client is the wife, and she's, you know, typically like 35 to 55 years old with kids. And to be honest, she probably doesn't want to read about gum disease. It may be important if she has it that, you know, like that second or one of the kids do, but she doesn't want to read about it. She wants to go to you and she wants you to kind of fix that for her. But what she might want to read about is the seven tips for keeping her kids safe on Facebook. She might want to read about something that's happening in the local area that might be fun for her and her family to go do, you know, in a couple of weeks. She might want to read about how to train and run for a 5K. So figure out what's going to improve the life of the person who is reading the newsletter. And that's one that will get you pass around value, which is where they go and give it to the neighbor across the street. It will keep you top of mind. It will have them save the newsletter. You know, I mean, there's all sorts of benefits. Help others, again, like Zig said, help others get what they want. You'll get what you want. And so help make your average client's life better. Third, you're going to need content to entertain them. People do not want to be bored. And we all know entertainers make more than teachers. So you definitely want to entertain them. And then you also do need some business-relevant content. There's, you know, times.
